bug_data.creator: rob@robwu.nl
bug_data.description:
There have been [multiple reports in the past few releases](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1724360#c25) of langpacks getting disabled after a Firefox update. I found one possible reason today: if the add-on updater check runs after the browser+langpack update was staged, it may update the langpack for the currently running Firefox version, which triggers the removal of the staged langpack. Consequently, when Firefox finally updates, it does not have a compatible langpack, so the langpack is disabled (because we do not check for compatible langpacks on startup - bug 1995824).

A user updated from Firefox 145 to 146 on macOS, but langpacks were not updated to 146, thus disabled.
To debug this, I requested the following diagnostics information to build a timeline of events:

- In `about:support`, click on "Show Update History" to see the update history. I also asked for the contents of the "Update Folder" shown at `about:support`, specifically `last-update.log` and `backup-update.log` to get more details on update behavior.
   - This showed that the update happened today at 13:00.
- In the macOS terminal, run `log show --predicate 'eventMessage contains "firefox"' --last 8h` to get the system logs of the last 8 hours, including the time of the update. In these logs, I searched for lines containing "Successfully spawned firefox" to see when Firefox was actually (re)started.
  - This showed two times: the time of the first Firefox startup, and the time of the Firefox startup after the update.
- I asked for the `extensions.json` file in the profile directory (directory can be found in `about:support`, "Profile Folder")
  - I looked for the `updateDate` field of the langpack of interest (timestamp in ms).
- I asked to visit `about:config` and look for the `app.update.lastUpdateTime.addon-background-update-timer` preference value.
  - This is the timestamp in seconds of the most recent add-on update check.

The result is as follows (ordered chronologically):

|time|data/message|data source|description|
|-|-|-|-|
|2025-12-16 13:00:34.312058+0200 | Successfully spawned firefox[87025] | macOS log | startup of Firefox 145 |
|2025-12-16 13:00:45+0200 | Performing a staged update | last-update.log or backup-update.log | download Firefox 146 update |
|2025-12-16 13:01:07+0200 | 1765882867 | about:config | value of addon-background-update-timer |
|2025-12-16 13:01:07.743+0200 | `"updateDate": 1765882867743` | extensions.json prettified | langpack updated for version 145 |
|2025-12-16 13:07:15.219541+0200 | Successfully spawned firefox[87122] | macOS log| startup of Firefox 146 |

This shows that between the staging of the Firefox update (which includes the staging of the langpack for that version, 146), the add-on background updater ran, which updated the langpack for the currently running version (145). This action results in the loss of the staged langpack, which manifests itself at the next browser restart as the langpack getting disabled (for it being incompatible).

comments.author: rob@robwu.nl
comments.creator: rob@robwu.nl
rob@robwu.nl
comments.author: rob@robwu.nl
comments.raw_text:
The race condition occurs between the following two uses of a langpack in the "staged" directory:
- Regular add-on installation: https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/911b3eec6c5e58a9a49e23aa105e49aa76e00f9c/toolkit/mozapps/extensions/internal/XPIInstall.sys.mjs#1923-1930,2031-2033
  * (if postponed, which I believe we don't do for langpacks: https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/911b3eec6c5e58a9a49e23aa105e49aa76e00f9c/toolkit/mozapps/extensions/internal/XPIInstall.sys.mjs#2130-2135 )
- Langpack staging ahead of updates: https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/911b3eec6c5e58a9a49e23aa105e49aa76e00f9c/toolkit/mozapps/update/UpdateService.sys.mjs#6262

The race observed in this bug is that "Langpack staging" and "Regular add-on installation" happens after each other.
In theory, it is also possible for these two events to happen concurrently. This would be a relatively rare race condition, but also cause bad failures.

A potential approach to resolve this is to detect whether langpack staging has happened, and if so, prevent updates to langpacks in this session. The downside of that is that if the current session already had broken langpacks that would be fixed by an update, that these langpacks would not install. It would also be challenging to account for the "relatively rare race condition" mentioned above.

Another approach would be to use separate staging directories (or file names) to avoid conflicts between the two. The downside to this is architectural and implementation complexity; staging does not only involve files, but also additional metadata in the add-on database.

Yet another approach is to keep a copy of the (small) metadata in memory, and temporarily rename the staged langpack xpi file before a new version of the current langpack is updated, then rename it back after.

Yet another approach is to keep a copy of the (small) metadata in memory, and temporarily rename the staged langpack xpi file before a new version of the current langpack is updated, then rename it back after.

comments.creator: phab-bot@bmo.tld
comments.author: phab-bot@bmo.tld
comments.text:
### firefox-release Uplift Approval Request
- **User impact if declined**: This patch ensures that the browser is correctly localized after a major browser update. Previously it was possible for the langpack to be lost, resulting in a poor user experience. Due to the bug, the version receiving the fix can still encounter the bug, it is future major version updates that benefit from the fix.

The patch has landed in Nightly 148, which means that users will stop experiencing the issue in 149+.

This upift targets 147 release, to make sure that users performing a major update to 148 won't encounter this bug.

It is too late for the 146 release, so version 147 will still be affected by this bug.
- **Code covered by automated testing**: yes
- **Fix verified in Nightly**: no
- **Needs manual QE test**: no
- **Steps to reproduce for manual QE testing**: 
- **Risk associated with taking this patch**: low
- **Explanation of risk level**: The problem is very well understood and covered by unit tests. This patch is designed to have minimal risk, only activating for langpacks staged during the application update process. It does not affect other operations and in the event of an unexpected interruption (e.g. browser crash), the failure mode is the same as before (old langpack still installed, and disabled if user updates to a new major version). Moreover the implementation explicitly allows langpacks for the current version to be installed.
- **String changes made/needed**: None
- **Is Android affected?**: no

Previously it was possible for the langpack to be lost, resulting in a poor user experience. Due to the bug, the version receiving the fix can still encounter the bug, it is future major version updates that benefit from the fix.

- **Code covered by automated testing**: yes

This patch is designed to have minimal risk, only activating for langpacks staged during the application update process. Moreover the implementation explicitly allows langpacks for the current version to be installed.

- **Is Android affected?**: no

comments.raw_text: We're fixing this in a 147 dot release, but it'd be better to relnote it in 148 as that'll be the first release where users will actually see the affect of this patch.
comments.text: We're fixing this in a 147 dot release, but it'd be better to relnote it in 148 as that'll be the first release where users will actually see the affect of this patch.

comments.creator: rob@robwu.nl
comments.author: rob@robwu.nl
comments.text:
(In reply to Ryan VanderMeulen [:RyanVM] from comment #8)
> We're fixing this in a 147 dot release, but it'd be better to relnote it in 148 as that'll be the first release where users will actually see the affect of this patch.

This issue can still happen if the user upgrades from a version before the fix to a major version regardless of the fix. E.g. 147.0 to 148, or even something like 146 to 148.

But with the many weeks between the first dot release and the next major release, the expectation is that most users who would be facing this bug will be saved by the fix here.
comments.raw_text:
(In reply to Ryan VanderMeulen [:RyanVM] from comment #8)

This issue can still happen if the user upgrades from a version before the fix to a major version regardless of the fix. E.g. 147.0 to 148, or even something like 146 to 148.

But with the many weeks between the first dot release and the next major release, the expectation is that most users who would be facing this bug will be saved by the fix here.

bug_data.summary: Langpack can be disabled after a Firefox update, if the current langpack was updated between staging of the update+langpack and restarting

comments.text:
There have been [multiple reports in the past few releases](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1724360#c25) of langpacks getting disabled after a Firefox update. I found one possible reason today: if the add-on updater check runs after the browser+langpack update was staged, it may update the langpack for the currently running Firefox version, which triggers the removal of the staged langpack. Consequently, when Firefox finally updates, it does not have a compatible langpack, so the langpack is disabled (because we do not check for compatible langpacks on startup - bug 1995824).

A user updated from Firefox 145 to 146 on macOS, but langpacks were not updated to 146, thus disabled.
To debug this, I requested the following diagnostics information to build a timeline of events:

- In `about:support`, click on "Show Update History" to see the update history. I also asked for the contents of the "Update Folder" shown at `about:support`, specifically `last-update.log` and `backup-update.log` to get more details on update behavior.
   - This showed that the update happened today at 13:00.
- In the macOS terminal, run `log show --predicate 'eventMessage contains "firefox"' --last 8h` to get the system logs of the last 8 hours, including the time of the update. In these logs, I searched for lines containing "Successfully spawned firefox" to see when Firefox was actually (re)started.
  - This showed two times: the time of the first Firefox startup, and the time of the Firefox startup after the update.
- I asked for the `extensions.json` file in the profile directory (directory can be found in `about:support`, "Profile Folder")
  - I looked for the `updateDate` field of the langpack of interest (timestamp in ms).
- I asked to visit `about:config` and look for the `app.update.lastUpdateTime.addon-background-update-timer` preference value.
  - This is the timestamp in seconds of the most recent add-on update check.

The result is as follows (ordered chronologically):

|time|data/message|data source|description|
|-|-|-|-|
|2025-12-16 13:00:34.312058+0200 | Successfully spawned firefox[87025] | macOS log | startup of Firefox 145 |
|2025-12-16 13:00:45+0200 | Performing a staged update | last-update.log or backup-update.log | download Firefox 146 update |
|2025-12-16 13:01:07+0200 | 1765882867 | about:config | value of addon-background-update-timer |
|2025-12-16 13:01:07.743+0200 | `"updateDate": 1765882867743` | extensions.json prettified | langpack updated for version 145 |
|2025-12-16 13:07:15.219541+0200 | Successfully spawned firefox[87122] | macOS log| startup of Firefox 146 |

This shows that between the staging of the Firefox update (which includes the staging of the langpack for that version, 146), the add-on background updater ran, which updated the langpack for the currently running version (145). This action results in the loss of the staged langpack, which manifests itself at the next browser restart as the langpack getting disabled (for it being incompatible).

comments.raw_text:
The race condition occurs between the following two uses of a langpack in the "staged" directory:
- Regular add-on installation: https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/911b3eec6c5e58a9a49e23aa105e49aa76e00f9c/toolkit/mozapps/extensions/internal/XPIInstall.sys.mjs#1923-1930,2031-2033
  * (if postponed, which I believe we don't do for langpacks: https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/911b3eec6c5e58a9a49e23aa105e49aa76e00f9c/toolkit/mozapps/extensions/internal/XPIInstall.sys.mjs#2130-2135 )
- Langpack staging ahead of updates: https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/911b3eec6c5e58a9a49e23aa105e49aa76e00f9c/toolkit/mozapps/update/UpdateService.sys.mjs#6262

The race observed in this bug is that "Langpack staging" and "Regular add-on installation" happens after each other.
In theory, it is also possible for these two events to happen concurrently. This would be a relatively rare race condition, but also cause bad failures.

A potential approach to resolve this is to detect whether langpack staging has happened, and if so, prevent updates to langpacks in this session. The downside of that is that if the current session already had broken langpacks that would be fixed by an update, that these langpacks would not install. It would also be challenging to account for the "relatively rare race condition" mentioned above.

Another approach would be to use separate staging directories (or file names) to avoid conflicts between the two. The downside to this is architectural and implementation complexity; staging does not only involve files, but also additional metadata in the add-on database.

Yet another approach is to keep a copy of the (small) metadata in memory, and temporarily rename the staged langpack xpi file before a new version of the current langpack is updated, then rename it back after.

comments.text:
Created attachment 9535460
Bug 2006489 - Keep staged langpack despite other installation


Original Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D276856

### firefox-release Uplift Approval Request
- **User impact if declined**: This patch ensures that the browser is correctly localized after a major browser update. Previously it was possible for the langpack to be lost, resulting in a poor user experience. Due to the bug, the version receiving the fix can still encounter the bug, it is future major version updates that benefit from the fix.

The patch has landed in Nightly 148, which means that users will stop experiencing the issue in 149+.

This upift targets 147 release, to make sure that users performing a major update to 148 won't encounter this bug.

It is too late for the 146 release, so version 147 will still be affected by this bug.
- **Code covered by automated testing**: yes
- **Fix verified in Nightly**: no
- **Needs manual QE test**: no
- **Steps to reproduce for manual QE testing**: 
- **Risk associated with taking this patch**: low
- **Explanation of risk level**: The problem is very well understood and covered by unit tests. This patch is designed to have minimal risk, only activating for langpacks staged during the application update process. It does not affect other operations and in the event of an unexpected interruption (e.g. browser crash), the failure mode is the same as before (old langpack still installed, and disabled if user updates to a new major version). Moreover the implementation explicitly allows langpacks for the current version to be installed.
- **String changes made/needed**: None
- **Is Android affected?**: no
comments.creator: phab-bot@bmo.tld

comments.text: We're fixing this in a 147 dot release, but it'd be better to relnote it in 148 as that'll be the first release where users will actually see the affect of this patch.

comments.text:
(In reply to Ryan VanderMeulen [:RyanVM] from comment #8)
> We're fixing this in a 147 dot release, but it'd be better to relnote it in 148 as that'll be the first release where users will actually see the affect of this patch.

This issue can still happen if the user upgrades from a version before the fix to a major version regardless of the fix. E.g. 147.0 to 148, or even something like 146 to 148.

But with the many weeks between the first dot release and the next major release, the expectation is that most users who would be facing this bug will be saved by the fix here.

crashes.signature: AsyncShutdownTimeout | IOUtils: waiting for sendTelemetry IO to complete | TelemetryController: shutting down

bug_analysis.root_cause: A race condition where the add-on updater runs after a Firefox update and langpack update are staged but before the browser restart, causing the langpack for the currently running version to be updated and the staged langpack to be removed, resulting in no compatible langpack after update.
symptoms: Langpack is disabled after a Firefox update
Browser is not localized correctly after update
reproduction_steps: Update Firefox from one major version to another (e.g., 145 to 146) on macOS
Allow the add-on background updater to run after the staged update but before browser restart
Restart Firefox and observe that the langpack is disabled due to incompatibility
bug_analysis.technical_details: Between staging a Firefox update (including the langpack for the new version) and restarting the browser, the add-on background updater runs and updates the langpack for the currently running Firefox version. This update triggers removal of the staged langpack for the new version. On restart, Firefox lacks a compatible langpack and disables it due to missing compatibility checks on startup (related to bug 1995824).
bug_analysis.user_impact: Users experience loss of language localization after updating Firefox, resulting in a poor user experience with disabled langpacks.

refined_analysis.root_cause: A race condition occurs when AddonUpdateChecker.sys.mjs runs the add-on background updater after a Firefox update is staged but before browser restart. The updater refreshes langpacks for the running Firefox version, deleting the staged langpack for the new version and causing langpack incompatibility on restart.
reproduction_steps: Update Firefox from one major version to another (e.g., 145 → 146) on macOS
Allow AddonUpdateChecker.sys.mjs background updater to run after staging but before restart
Restart Firefox and observe disabled langpack due to incompatibility
refined_analysis.technical_details: After a Firefox update stages the new version’s langpack, AddonUpdateChecker.sys.mjs triggers a background update that refreshes langpacks for the running version. This removes the staged langpack files for the new version, causing Firefox to disable localization on restart due to validation failures.
refined_analysis.user_impact: Users lose localization support after updating Firefox, resulting in disabled langpacks and degraded user experience.
refined_analysis.proposed_fix_approach: In AddonUpdateChecker.sys.mjs, add a guard around the langpack update logic (e.g., in the updateLangpack function or equivalent) to detect when a Firefox update is staged. Defer langpack updates until after the browser restart to prevent overwriting staged langpacks during this critical window.
refined_analysis.optimized_context: In toolkit/mozapps/extensions/internal/AddonUpdateChecker.sys.mjs, the background updater refreshes langpacks after a Firefox update is staged but before restart. This erroneously removes the staged langpack for the new version, causing localization to fail on restart. Adding a guard in the langpack update code path (likely updateLangpack) to skip updates if a new Firefox version is staged will preserve staged langpacks and maintain localization compatibility.
refined_analysis.refinement_critique: The current analysis clearly identifies the regression’s root cause, symptoms, and impact, and includes reproduction steps and affected components, which is good. However, it is verbose and somewhat repetitive, which reduces conciseness. It lacks explicit references to the specific functions or code paths inside AddonUpdateChecker.sys.mjs responsible for the race condition, which would improve artifact hints. The proposed fix is conceptually sound but vague about where exactly to add the guard. The optimized_context is too long and repeats details from the root cause and technical details rather than focusing on pinpointing the exact timing window and file locations critical to the bug. Overall, it needs tighter scope, removal of redundant info, and more precise, actionable pointers to relevant code areas for AI to generate an effective fix.

filtered_context.concise_summary: After a Firefox update is staged, AddonUpdateChecker.sys.mjs runs a background langpack refresh for the running version before restart, which deletes the staged langpack for the new version. This causes the langpack to be disabled on restart due to validation failure. Adding a guard in AddonUpdateChecker.sys.mjs (likely in updateLangpack) to skip langpack updates if a Firefox update is staged will prevent overwriting staged langpacks and preserve localization.
technical_constraints: Background langpack update runs after update staging but before restart
Langpack updates must be deferred if a new Firefox version is staged
Guard logic should reside in AddonUpdateChecker.sys.mjs updateLangpack or equivalent
